Bead: ddx-7aa810b8 — Track execute-bead worktree lifecycle Verdict: no_changes. The bead is multi-surface and the AC set cannot be completed to the specified quality in a single pass without committing partial or red code across several surfaces. Per the bead instructions ("a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit"), this attempt returns for breakdown/re-queue with a stronger budget or a pre-split bead set. What the AC demands (11 items, summarized): 1. GraphQL resolver executeBeadWorktrees(projectId) returning {path, branch, beadId?, executionId?, baseRev, headRev, state, createdAt, lastActivityAt} with unit tests for all four states. 2. Pure, table-driven state classifier (active | completed | abandoned | orphan) with ≥2 edge cases per state. 3. New SvelteKit route /nodes//projects/

/worktrees — sortable, filterable table. 4. Row actions (Remove, Force-remove) wired through GraphQL mutations with confirm-gating and typed confirmation for force-remove. 5. CLI: `ddx server state worktree-prune [--dry-run] [--project] [--yes]` with an integration test against a fixture repo containing one of each state. 6. Server-side sweep with configurable interval (default 15m), a GraphQL counter rollup type, and opt-in `worktrees.auto_prune` config. 7. Cross-links to /executions/ (depends on ddx-726bc5f2) and a reciprocal "Worktree" section on the execution detail page. 8. Fixture seed with ≥100 worktrees across states; p95 ≤ 200ms. 9. Playwright e2e that seeds one of each state, asserts state badges, exercises Remove confirm flow, and asserts active-worktree gating. 10. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ for Part 4 canonical path convention + migration plan (required only if Part 4 ships). 11. Cross-reference notes to ddx-12cae4dd, ddx-71fbe968, ddx-726bc5f2, ddx-15f7ee0b. What is in place already (checked): - Server package cli/internal/server/ with graphql resolver module, state.go / state_executions.go, workers.go, and a land_coordinator — the sweep / state-classification pattern has prior art here. - Frontend SvelteKit tree includes /nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId] with executions, workers, beads, documents, etc. No worktrees route yet. - Executions detail page already exists at .../executions/[executionId]. Cross-link target in AC 7 is therefore implementable, not blocked on ddx-726bc5f2 (which may itself be partial or separate). - Two worktree path conventions confirmed at runtime: .claude/worktrees/agent-/ (branch-based, older) /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx---/ (detached) This execution itself is running from the /tmp convention. What is blocking a single-pass completion: - The surface area spans Go (resolver + mutations + sweep + classifier + CLI subcommand + config plumbing), SvelteKit (new route, table with sort/filter, two confirm dialogs, mutation wiring, Houdini type regeneration), fixtures (≥100-worktree seeder), unit tests, an integration test, and a Playwright e2e. Each surface has its own test-green bar; shipping them all together green within one pass risks red commits on the surfaces that get squeezed last. - AC 5 (CLI subcommand) requires a new `ddx server state` command group; the current CLI surface exposes `ddx server` but does not have a `state` subcommand tree — adding one is a small but independent surface that interacts with the wider command layout. - AC 6 (sweep + counter + config) needs config-schema changes (.ddx/config.yaml worktrees block), which touches the config loader and every test that loads config. This is a cross-cutting change and deserves its own attempt slot. - AC 8's ≥100-worktree fixture is cheap to generate but the p95 claim must be measured against a real handler — per the user's standing perf stance (feedback_testing_and_performance), a perf claim requires baseline + boundary + harness, not just a seed and a one-shot timing. Recommended breakdown (for re-queue): A. Inventory resolver + pure classifier + unit tests. (AC 1, AC 2; pure, no UI, no config changes.) B. Worktrees route + list view + cross-links to executions. (AC 3, AC 7; depends on A; no mutations yet.) C. Remove / Force-remove mutations + row actions + Playwright e2e. (AC 4, AC 9; depends on B.) D. CLI worktree-prune subcommand + integration test. (AC 5; can run parallel to C once A is done.) E. Sweep + counter + config + opt-in auto_prune. (AC 6; depends on A; config-surface change.) F. ≥100-worktree fixture + perf harness with p95 measurement. (AC 8; depends on A; separate because of the perf methodology bar.) G. Path-convention design note + Part 4 migration plan. (AC 10, AC 11 for cross-refs; docs-only.) A follow-up attempt should take A alone, or A+B together, to stay within a single pass's green-bar budget. No files were modified in this worktree. rationale: Bead: ddx-7aa810b8 — Track execute-bead worktree lifecycle Verdict: no_changes.
